title: Didiclis ornithopodioides
taxonomy: Didiclis ornithopodioides is classified as a fossil plant species. It belongs to the family of extinct flowering plants and is thought to be closely related to the family of modern-day Proteaceae. Further classification places it within the order Proteales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of Proteaceae, Nelumbonaceae, and Platanaceae. The classification of Didiclis ornithopodioides is based on its fossilized remains and comparative anatomy with other flowering plant species. This classification helps scientists to better understand the evolutionary history of flowering plants and their relationships with each other.
